userStories.title: Add escalateOnExhaustion config field
userStories.description: Add the `escalateOnExhaustion` boolean field to `RectificationConfig` and wire it through the config schema, defaults, and CLI descriptions. This field controls whether the rectification loop escalates to a higher model tier after exhausting `maxRetries`. It defaults to `true` so existing users get the benefit without opt-in. The Zod schema must accept the field as optional (absent = true). The config-descriptions entry must explain that this triggers a one-step model tier escalation (e.g., balanced → powerful) when all retries are exhausted with remaining failures.
acceptanceCriteria: RectificationConfig in src/config/runtime-types.ts has a field `escalateOnExhaustion` typed as `boolean`
RectificationConfigSchema in src/config/schemas.ts includes `escalateOnExhaustion: z.boolean().optional().default(true)` so omitting the field in project config parses as `true`
DEFAULT_CONFIG.execution.rectification.escalateOnExhaustion equals `true` in src/config/defaults.ts
FIELD_DESCRIPTIONS in src/cli/config-descriptions.ts has a key `execution.rectification.escalateOnExhaustion` whose value contains the phrase 'model tier escalation'

When a project config sets `execution.rectification.escalateOnExhaustion` to `false` and is parsed through the config loader, the resulting NaxConfig has `execution.rectification.escalateOnExhaustion === false`

userStories.title: Log failing test names in rectification
userStories.description: Enhance the structured log entry emitted when rectification detects remaining failures after an attempt to include the names of the failing tests. The `failingTests` field should hold up to 10 test name strings (format: `testName` from `TestFailure`). When more than 10 tests fail, cap `failingTests` at 10 and add a `totalFailingTests` field with the full count. When the parser returns no structured failures (only a count), `failingTests` should be an empty array and `totalFailingTests` should reflect `testSummary.failed`. This change is confined to the existing `logger?.warn` call at the end of each retry iteration inside `runRectificationLoop`.
acceptanceCriteria: When rectification detects remaining failures after an attempt and there are 10 or fewer structured TestFailure entries, the log entry at warn level includes a `failingTests` field containing an array of all their `testName` strings

When rectification detects more than 10 structured TestFailure entries, the log entry includes `failingTests` with only the first 10 testName strings and `totalFailingTests` equal to the full `testSummary.failed` count
When parseBunTestOutput returns an empty `failures` array but `failed > 0`, the log entry includes `failingTests: []` and `totalFailingTests` equal to `testSummary.failed`
When there are 10 or fewer failures, `totalFailingTests` is not present in the log entry (or equals `failingTests.length` — consistent with not being a truncation indicator)

userStories.title: Implement rectification model escalation
userStories.description: After the rectification retry loop exhausts `maxRetries` with failures still remaining, and when `rectification.escalateOnExhaustion` is `true` and `autoMode.escalation.enabled` is `true`, invoke the agent one additional time using the next tier from `autoMode.escalation.tierOrder`. The escalated attempt uses the same `runVerification` path as normal retries. If the escalated attempt passes verification, `runRectificationLoop` returns `true`. If it fails, the function returns `false` and logs a warning. When the current tier is already the last entry in `tierOrder`, no escalation fires. When `escalateOnExhaustion` is `false`, the existing behavior is unchanged. The `_rectificationDeps` object must have `escalateTier` added so tests can stub it. The escalated attempt must count as exactly one additional agent invocation (total = `maxRetries + 1`).
acceptanceCriteria: When `rectification.escalateOnExhaustion` is `true`, `autoMode.escalation.enabled` is `true`, and the while loop exits with `rectificationState.attempt >= maxRetries` and `rectificationState.currentFailures > 0`, `runRectificationLoop` invokes the agent once more with the tier returned by `escalateTier(currentTier, tierOrder)`
When the current model tier is `'balanced'` and `tierOrder` is `[{tier:'fast',...},{tier:'balanced',...},{tier:'powerful',...}]`, the escalated agent.run call receives `modelTier: 'powerful'`
When the current model tier is the last entry in `tierOrder` (escalateTier returns null), no additional agent.run call is made and `runRectificationLoop` returns `false`

When the escalated agent.run succeeds and the subsequent `runVerification` call returns `success: true`, `runRectificationLoop` returns `true` and logs an info-level message containing both the original tier name and the escalated tier name

When the escalated agent.run succeeds but the subsequent `runVerification` call returns `success: false`, `runRectificationLoop` returns `false` and logs a warn-level message containing the phrase 'escalated rectification also failed'
When `rectification.escalateOnExhaustion` is `false`, `runRectificationLoop` does not invoke the agent after the while loop exits and returns `false` as before
When the while loop exits early due to `abortOnIncreasingFailures` (attempt < maxRetries), no escalation fires because the attempt count guard `attempt >= maxRetries` is false
The total number of agent.run invocations across the while loop and escalation path when escalation triggers equals `maxRetries + 1`

userStories.title: Include prior attempt context in escalated prompt
userStories.description: When the escalated rectification attempt runs, the prompt sent to the agent must include a 'Previous Rectification Attempts' section that tells the agent how many prior attempts were made, what tier they used, and which specific tests are still failing. This gives the higher-tier model context to try a different approach rather than repeating the same surface-level patch. Add a new exported function `createEscalatedRectificationPrompt` in `src/verification/rectification.ts` that builds this prompt. When there are more than 10 failing test names, include only the first 10 followed by a line of the form 'and N more'. The prompt must also include a line indicating the model was escalated (e.g., 'escalated from balanced to powerful').
acceptanceCriteria: When `createEscalatedRectificationPrompt` is called, the returned string contains a section header matching 'Previous Rectification Attempts'
The returned prompt includes the count of prior attempts and the original model tier name in the prior-attempts section
When the `failures` array has 10 or fewer entries, the prompt includes each entry's `testName` in a list under the failing tests section
When the `failures` array has more than 10 entries, the prompt includes the first 10 test names and a line of the form 'and N more' where N is the remaining count
The returned prompt contains a line indicating escalation direction with both the source tier and target tier (e.g., containing both 'balanced' and 'powerful' when escalating between those tiers)

The escalated prompt produced by `createEscalatedRectificationPrompt` is used by `runRectificationLoop` as the prompt for the escalated agent.run call (not the standard `createRectificationPrompt` output)

analysis:
## Codebase Analysis

### Key Files

**Config layer (US-001):**
- `src/config/runtime-types.ts` (lines 36-47): `RectificationConfig` interface is missing `escalateOnExhaustion: boolean`. Add it after `abortOnIncreasingFailures`.
- `src/config/schemas.ts` (lines 54-60): `RectificationConfigSchema` Zod object needs `escalateOnExhaustion: z.boolean().optional().default(true)` added.
- `src/config/defaults.ts` (lines 54-59): `DEFAULT_CONFIG.execution.rectification` object needs `escalateOnExhaustion: true`.
- `src/cli/config-descriptions.ts` (line 73): Existing entry for `abortOnIncreasingFailures` — add sibling entry for `execution.rectification.escalateOnExhaustion` with text describing model tier escalation on retry exhaustion.
- `src/config/merge.ts`: Uses spread `...packageOverride.execution` which picks up nested rectification fields automatically — no change needed.

**Escalation utilities (already exist, reuse directly):**
- `src/execution/escalation/escalation.ts`: exports `escalateTier(currentTier, tierOrder)` which returns the next tier string or null — exactly what US-002 needs. Import via barrel `src/execution/escalation`.
- Tier derivation in `rectification-loop.ts` lines 84-86 uses `config.autoMode.complexityRouting?.[complexity]` — same logic must be used in escalation path to derive `currentTier`.

**Core loop (US-002, US-003, US-004):**
- `src/verification/rectification-loop.ts`: Main target. The `while` loop (line 65) exhausts `maxRetries`. After the loop body (lines 157-171), returns `false`. Escalation must be inserted between the existing post-loop log and the final `return false`. The `_rectificationDeps` injectable object (lines 39-42) provides `getAgent` and `runVerification` — add `escalateTier` here for testability. The `agentGetFn ?? _rectificationDeps.getAgent` pattern at line 76 must be reused for the escalated attempt.
- `src/verification/rectification.ts`: `createRectificationPrompt()` (lines 54-105) — US-003 needs a new exported function `createEscalatedRectificationPrompt()` here that accepts prior attempt count, original tier, escalated tier, and failing test names, producing the augmented prompt.
- `src/verification/types.ts`: `TestFailure` has `file`, `testName`, `error`, `stackTrace`. For US-004 logging and US-003 prompt, extract `testName` from the failures array. No changes to this file.
- `src/execution/test-output-parser.ts`: Is a re-export shim pointing to `src/verification/parser.ts` — no changes needed.

### Architecture Decisions

1. **Where escalation lives:** After the `while` loop in `runRectificationLoop`, gated by `rectificationConfig.escalateOnExhaustion && config.autoMode.escalation.enabled && rectificationState.currentFailures > 0`. Must NOT fire when abortOnIncreasingFailures triggered early exit (check attempt count equals maxRetries).
2. **Tier derivation:** Reuse the same 3-line pattern at lines 84-86 of rectification-loop.ts to get `currentTier`, then call `escalateTier()` from the escalation barrel.
3. **Injectable deps:** Add `escalateTier` to `_rectificationDeps` so tests can stub it without touching globals. Also add the escalated attempt's agent.run call path through `agentGetFn ?? _rectificationDeps.getAgent`.
4. **Cost:** `agentResult.estimatedCost` is already logged — no accumulation needed since the function returns `boolean`. The log line satisfies AC-7 (cost not leaked/silent).
5. **Total invocations:** maxRetries attempts in the while loop + 1 escalated attempt = maxRetries + 1 total. The while loop guard `state.attempt >= config.maxRetries` already ensures the loop stops at maxRetries.
6. **Escalated prompt:** New function in `src/verification/rectification.ts` accepting `(failures: TestFailure[], story: UserStory, config: RectificationConfig, priorAttempts: number, fromTier: string, toTier: string)` — builds the base prompt then appends the 'Previous Rectification Attempts' section.
7. **US-004 log enhancement:** In the existing `logger?.warn('rectification', '${label} still failing after attempt', {...})` call at line 150-154, add `failingTests: testSummary.failures.slice(0, 10).map(f => f.testName)` and `totalFailingTests: testSummary.failed` to the structured log object.

### Risks
- `rectification-loop.ts` is touched by both US-002 and US-004. Stories must be sequenced: US-004 first or merged. Recommended: run US-004 before US-002 to avoid merge conflicts.
- The escalation path duplicates the agent.run call from inside the while loop. Keep the duplication explicit (no premature abstraction) — the escalated call has different prompt and modelTier.
- `abortOnIncreasingFailures` may cause the while loop to exit before reaching `maxRetries`. The escalation guard must check `rectificationState.attempt >= rectificationConfig.maxRetries` (not just `currentFailures > 0`) to avoid firing on early-exit scenarios. Actually re-reading: when abortOnIncreasingFailures fires, `attempt < maxRetries`, so the guard `attempt >= maxRetries` correctly prevents escalation on regression-spiral exit.
- `mergePackageConfig` deep-spreads `execution.rectification` via `...packageOverride.execution` which only does a shallow merge of `rectification`. The nested `rectification` object is fully replaced if present in packageOverride — this is existing behavior and `escalateOnExhaustion` inherits it without change.
